Details: Granular activated carbon can achieve very high removals of PCE (up to greater than 99.9 percent removal). These results include data from pilot and  full-scale sites, including remediation sites with very high PCE concentrations (up to 170,000 mg/L) and from drinking water facilities with more moderate concentrations (3.2 to approximately 16 mg/L) [2308, 2411, 2431, 2432, 2433, 2434, 2435]. One full-scale study of drinking water treatment found GAC maintained very high removal (greater than 90 percent) of PCE for more than 8 months, declining to less than 50 percent only after 20 months [2435]. USEPA has identified granular activated carbon as a Best Available Technology for removal of PCE from drinking water [1250].
